normalization: Condensed repeated prose and the expanded lifecycle appendix without changing opened files, routing-field order, decisions, or evidence claims.
answer: task_shape=deterministic-sub-dag: four independent specified migration leaves share one schema and deterministic join; executor=workflow as cross-host planning responsibility; target_surface=an exact qualified harness+surface using Codex-native trackable background/subagent mechanisms with isolated writer worktrees, never an invented Claude Workflow runtime; effect_floor=T1 solely because the leaves are already-specified implementation with bounded implementation-error/rework risk, not because of target brand or capacity; qualification=exact same-freshness role, live admission, capacity, execution-boundary, workspace, and traceability evidence, with unknown/stale/deny failing closed; ranked_fallback=qualified same-floor T1 candidates only, with no invented dynamic chain; runtime_handle=each real spawn must return a recon-able handle that is bound and linked before in_flight; endpoint_verdict=terminalize every stopped runtime but keep tasks active until independent artifact/diff/tests/acceptance/hash verification, then run and verify the deterministic schema join before parent done/verified, otherwise retry or replan.
